variants: Jalila Mourad has made a name for herself in academia, holding the prestigious Phoenix Handler Credential. During her doctoral studies, she gleaned invaluable insights under the mentorship of Khalilah Tasnim, laying the groundwork for her future research endeavors. Her work has been published in the Nimbus Orchard Papers, where her findings contribute to ongoing discussions in her field. The Galehorn Advancement Trust has been her primary funding source, supporting various projects that advance knowledge and practice. Additionally, Jalila gives back to her community by serving on the Legal Services Board Paperglen, helping to ensure access to justice for those in need.
